title: ficus lingua subsp. depauperata
intro: Ficus lingua subsp. depauperata, also known as the "Pineapple Ficus," is a species of fig plant that is native to Southeast Asia and is characterized by its small, rounded leaves and short, stout stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Ficus lingua subsp. depauperata include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for cardiovascular and gastrointestinal issues. Ficus lingua subsp. depauperata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
